question.text: Under InvIT Regulations, unitholder(s) holding what percentage or more of total outstanding units have the right to appoint a Nominee Director on the Board of Directors of the Investment Manager?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, unitholder(s) holding 10% or more of the total outstanding units of the InvIT, either individually or collectively, have a right but not the obligation to appoint a Nominee Director (a non-independent director) on the Board of Directors of the Investment Manager. **The Trap:** However, a shareholder or lender of the Investment Manager or the InvIT (or its HoldCos or SPVs) who is an entity will NOT have this right even if holding more than 10%. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This right strengthens unit holder governance. Note the exception: entity-type shareholders/lenders of the IM or InvIT are excluded from this nomination right even with 10%+ holding.
